seoTitle: How to Indent in Google Docs: Tab, Ruler & Hanging
metaDescription: Learn to indent paragraphs in Google Docs using the Tab key, toolbar buttons, the document ruler, and hanging indents for bibliography pages.
intro: Proper paragraph alignment matters for academic papers, reports, and reference lists alike. This guide covers the quick Tab-key method for first-line indents, the toolbar buttons for shifting whole blocks of text, and the ruler and Indentation Options for precise hanging indents.

steps.heading: Use Tab for a quick first-line indent
steps.body: Press the Tab key at the start of a paragraph to apply a standard half-inch indentation.

steps.heading: Shift whole blocks with toolbar buttons
steps.body: Use the Increase Indent and Decrease Indent buttons on the toolbar to move entire blocks of text at once.

steps.heading: Turn on the document ruler
steps.body: Activate the ruler from the View menu to get precise manual control over spacing markers.

steps.heading: Adjust the ruler markers
steps.body: Distinguish the First Line Indent marker (top rectangle) from the Left Indent marker (bottom triangle) and drag them to set custom spacing.

steps.heading: Set up hanging indents
steps.body: Use the Format menu's Indentation Options to configure hanging indents needed for bibliography or reference list pages.

faq.question: What's the fastest way to indent a paragraph in Google Docs?
faq.answer: Pressing Tab at the beginning of a paragraph applies a quick half-inch first-line indent.

faq.question: How do I create a hanging indent for a bibliography?
faq.answer: Use the Format menu's Indentation Options to set up a hanging indent, which is the standard requirement for reference lists.

faq.question: What's the difference between the two ruler markers?
faq.answer: The top rectangle controls the first-line indent, while the bottom triangle controls the left indent for the rest of the paragraph.

faq.question: Will my indents stay consistent between desktop and mobile?
faq.answer: Keeping your formatting consistent when switching devices can require a quick check, since spacing can occasionally shift between platforms.
